preview_text:
The model favours Iran at 49%, with Egypt positioned as the underdog at 22%. This represents a substantial gap: Iran's assessed winning chance is more than twice Egypt's, reflecting a meaningful quality differential in the statistical forecast.

The win-draw-loss spread suggests a competitive but asymmetric match. A draw sits at 30% and remains plausible, but Iran's advantage is clear enough that draws are not the likeliest outcome. The 27 percentage-point gap between Iran's win probability and Egypt's underscores that the model expects Iran to control most scenarios, though Egypt retains genuine chances to upset.

Model and market diverge markedly on this fixture. The market consensus prices Egypt's chances at 42% and Iran's at 27%—a near-reversal of the model's view. Polymarket prices align closely with sportsbooks. The dashboard makes no claim about which source better captures the true probabilities; the divergence is substantial enough to flag for users monitoring this match.
